name: ODYSSEY-X86J4125
description: Intel Celeron J4125 x86 SBC with onboard Microchip ATSAMD21 Arduino co-MCU, dual Gigabit Ethernet, RPi-compatible 40-pin header

model.comment: Sold in two SKUs: X86J4125800 (8GB RAM, no eMMC) and X86J4125864 (8GB RAM + 64GB eMMC). v1 board uses ATSAMD21 Arduino co-MCU and dual Intel I211AT 1GbE; v2 (sold separately under different SKUs) swaps to RP2040 + dual Intel I226-V 2.5GbE. This entry covers v1.

release-date.comment: Announced April 2021 as a faster J4125 update to the original ODYSSEY-X86J4105 (2019).

status.comment: Verified 2026-04-26: X86J4125800 listed at $218 but Out of Stock on Seeed; X86J4125864 marked Discontinued. v2 confirmed discontinued in the Seeed forum (Sept 2025). Still findable at third-party resellers (Amazon, RobotShop, DigiKey).

open-source-hardware.comment: Schematics/PCB design files not published; users requesting schematics in the Seeed forum did not receive an OSHW release. Unlike Seeed's reComputer/Jetson carriers, this board is not part of their OSHW lineup.

country-of-origin.comment: Seeed Studio is headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

cpu-architecture.value: Intel Celeron J4125 (Gemini Lake Refresh, 4× Goldmont Plus cores)

process-node.comment: Gemini Lake Refresh is Intel 14 nm.

opengl-es-version.comment: Gen9.5 UHD 600 supports OpenGL 4.5 and OpenGL ES 3.2 on the i915 mainline driver.

vulkan-support.comment: Intel UHD 600 (Gen9.5) supports Vulkan 1.3 via the open-source Mesa ANV driver.

npu.comment: Gemini Lake Refresh has no dedicated NPU.

hw-video-decode.comment: UHD 600 supports H.264, H.265 (HEVC) 10-bit, and VP9 hardware decode. No AV1 decode (predates Tiger Lake).

hw-video-encode.comment: QuickSync H.264 and H.265 encode supported via Gen9.5 media engine.

ram-min-gb.comment: Only an 8 GB configuration is sold; RAM is soldered LPDDR4 and not user-upgradeable.

emmc.comment: 64 GB eMMC v5.1 on the X86J4125864 SKU only; the X86J4125800 SKU ships without eMMC.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 Key M (2242/2280) exposes PCIe 2.0 ×4 and accepts NVMe SSDs. A second M.2 Key B slot supports SATA III/USB 2.0/UIM.

sata.comment: Onboard SATA III connector with adjacent power headers, plus SATA III via the M.2 Key B slot.

ethernet.comment: v1 has 2× Intel I211AT Gigabit Ethernet. The v2 board (separate SKU) bumps both to 2.5GbE via Intel I226-V.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 3.1 Type-C; also accepts USB-C PD power input.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the Ethernet ports and no PoE add-on hat documented.

max-display-resolution.comment: 4096×2160 @ 60 Hz on both HDMI 2.0a and DP 1.2a.

mipi-dsi.comment: x86 SoC; no MIPI-DSI display connector exposed.

mipi-csi.comment: x86 SoC; no MIPI-CSI camera connector exposed.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin Raspberry Pi-compatible header. A separate 28-pin header drives the ATSAMD21 Arduino co-MCU.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header is electrically pin-compatible with the Raspberry Pi GPIO header.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pin-compatible electrically but the 110×110 mm board does not match the RPi mounting-hole pattern, lacks HAT EEPROM auto-config, and HATs sit awkwardly because of the larger footprint and 28-pin Arduino header next to the 40-pin one.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× M.2 Key M (PCIe 2.0 ×4) + 1× M.2 Key B (SATA/USB)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 Key B slot does not expose PCIe — SATA III/USB 2.0/UIM only.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART available on the 40-pin RPi header (and additional UART debug header).

power-input.comment: 5.5/2.1 mm DC jack accepts 12–19 V; USB-C alternative supports PD 5–12 V.

idle-power-w.comment: No credible measured idle figure published. Seeed forum threads explicitly note the absence of vendor power data; the J4125 SoC has a 10W TDP but board-level idle measurement was not found in primary or secondary sources (verified 2026-04-26).

load-power-w.comment: Same situation as idle: no measured load figure from a credible reviewer. SoC TDP is 10 W; whole-board draw will be higher under sustained load but is unverified.

cooling-required.comment: Board ships with a low-profile heatsink and a 4-pin PWM fan header; the official Re_Computer case bundle uses an active fan. Vendor wiring assumes active cooling under sustained load.

rtc-battery-header.comment: JST 1.0 connector for a CR2032 3V coin cell.

form-factor-class.comment: 110×110 mm sits between Pico-ITX (100×72) and Nano-ITX (120×120). Closer to Pico-ITX in spirit but not an exact match — vendor markets it as a custom 4×4 inch SBC; Pico-ITX is the nearest defined class.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Custom mounting pattern designed for the Re_Computer stackable case; not RPi-compatible.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published by Seeed and not measured in reviews surveyed (verified 2026-04-26). The Re_Computer enclosed bundle weighs noticeably more but is not a bare-board figure.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard Intel Gemini Lake platform — fully supported by mainline Linux on Ubuntu 20.04+ (HDMI, audio, USB, Ethernet, Bluetooth working). One historical caveat in early reviews: microSD detection issues on Ubuntu 20.04, since resolved in newer kernels.

official-os-image.comment: eMMC SKU ships pre-installed with Windows 10 Enterprise; Seeed wiki documents installing Ubuntu, Windows, and OpenWrt. Vendor does not maintain a custom Linux image — uses upstream installers.

distros-with-images.comment: As a generic x86_64 platform, any standard Linux distro installer works. Listed those formally documented by Seeed (Ubuntu) and trivially supported via standard amd64 ISOs (Debian). Not listing Armbian/Raspberry Pi OS (ARM-only). OpenWrt is supported per Seeed marketing but not a typical desktop image.

bootloader.comment: Standard AMI UEFI BIOS with legacy CSM available; F7 invokes Boot Manager.

long-term-support.comment: v2 confirmed discontinued (Sept 2025); v1 SKUs are out of stock. As an x86 platform Linux upstream support effectively continues regardless of vendor commitment, but Seeed has not announced a defined LTS window for this product line.

android-support.comment: No official Android image from Seeed. Android-x86 community builds may run but are not vendor-supported.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Base SKU X86J4125800 (8GB RAM, no eMMC) listed at $218 USD on Seeed (verified 2026-04-26, currently Out of Stock).

msrp-top-usd.comment: Top SKU X86J4125864 (8GB RAM + 64GB eMMC) listed at $238 USD on Seeed (verified 2026-04-26, marked Discontinued). Re_Computer-bundled SKUs were higher (~$269) at launch.

available-regions.comment: Seeed ships globally; also distributed via Amazon (NA), DigiKey (global), RobotShop (NA/EU), and Botland (EU).

typical-lead-time.comment: Both Seeed SKUs Out of Stock or Discontinued (verified 2026-04-26). Some inventory remains at third-party resellers but stock is winding down.

documentation-quality.comment: Seeed wiki covers getting-started, OS install, Arduino co-MCU usage, GPIO mapping, and BIOS update procedures. User manual PDF exists. Schematics not published, which keeps it short of 5.

community-size.comment: Vendor forum is active but with low traffic compared to RPi/Orange Pi/Radxa communities. Niche x86-SBC-with-Arduino product.

active-forum-discord.comment: Seeed Studio forum has a dedicated ODYSSEY Serials category with regular activity (most recent threads from 2025).

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official Re_Computer stackable case is the main first-party accessory. Generic 40-pin RPi HATs may bolt on electrically but mechanical fit is limited (see RPi HAT Compatible). Few dedicated third-party add-ons compared to RPi/Radxa ecosystems.
